11.10.sanskrit:
शबला वलीकघटितस्फटिकद्युतिभिर्हरिन्मणिमयी वलभी ।
प्रतिसद्म भाति यमुना मिलिता सितसौरसैन्धवपयोभिरिव॥ १० ॥
11.10.english:
Every house that glistened with its white emerald roof reinforced with
the dazzling blue diamond at the ridge had the semblance of the Yamuna
River mixing with the white water of the Ganges.
रनद िाभवष्यनदह नवभ्रमिा-निरमो जिस्य निलरावनलष।ु
नवकटा नवटङ्क-कृ ि-पनत्र-िनिाः कृिकेत्यवक्ष्य
ै
ि कर्ञ्चि िो॥11-11॥

11.11.sanskrit:
यदि नाभविष्यदिह विभ्रमतानियमो जनस्य निलयावलिषु ।
विकटा विटङ्ककृतपत्रिततिः कृतकेत्यवैक्ष्यत कथञ्चन नो॥ ११ ॥
11.11.english:
If the people in Vaikunta did not invariably (as a rule) suffer from
illusion, then they would never comprehend the charming images of the
birds in the several nests of the rows of houses as artificial; (they could verily
mistake them, to be real birds; such is the consummate art of the divine
sculptors).
नवनवधस्तलु ोपगि-नचत्र-पटी-स-ुनविाि-लनम्ब-मनण-दाम-गणाः।
शरणान्तरष्वे नधक-परूणिाः प्र-चकानस्त नवभ्रम इवावगळि॥्11-12॥

11.12.english:
The bunches of gem-garlands fastened to the multi-coloured cloth
pieces, suspended from the beams flooded nay even out-poured, the
splendorous homes with strange resplendence.
नवमला-कृनिाः प्रकृनिरवे महा-मनण-हेम-मय्यनिल-वस्त-ुमरी।
इह साविे स् कल-कानन्त-रसाः कनर्िोऽनधकोनक्तनभरिाः कनवनभाः॥11-13॥

11.13.sanskrit:
विमलाकृतिः प्रकृतिरेव महामणिहेममय्यखिलवस्तुमयी ।
इह सम्भवेत्सकलकान्तिरसः कथितोऽधिकोक्तिभिरतः कविभिः ॥ १३ ॥
11.13.english:
In this celestial city, Goddess Mahalakshmi of immaculate form, the
presiding deity of Primordial Matter, herself stands transformed in different
shapes and forms, priceless gems, gold and rest of the objects. Indeed, it is
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 171

no exaggeration if the divine bards overwhelmed with its extreme glory
portray its magnificence in grand-eloquent terms. (It is as real as reality
itself.)
िदिानद-काल-नमि-मनुक्त-रिाःे अगणय्य-शा-ुमिु -सङ्-ििाःे।
िगरम
ै
ह
य
ा-परुमसहं नि-मि ल् सनि प्रभोररमहो मनहमा॥11-14॥

11.14.english:
Even though the towns are replete with liberated Brahmas and others
from times immemorial, so specious is this (strange) tract of this magnificent
Metropolis (Vaikunta) that it never suffers from congestion (they are never
full: no population explosion). Indeed greatly astounding is the divine
majesty of Lord Narayana.
भविंनवभानि भवुिक
ै-पिाःे भवुि-त्रराद्भि
ु-गणुानि-शनर।
वर-मनन्दर-प्रकर-मध्य-गिंनिज-मडिल-स्थ-शनशमडिल-वि॥्11-15॥

11.15.english:
Amidst the cluster of such gorgeous palaces, glitters in all glory the
unique mansion of the chief Lord of all the worlds, ‘a miracle of rare device’
excelling outright the many many wonders in the three worlds like the disc of
the moon scintillating among the myriad twinkling stars.
स्वरनमनन्दरैव पनि-माि-करी गहृ-कम
य
रत्र कुरुिेसिुराम।्
अनप नकङ्करी-दश-शिी-मनहिा नश्ररमत्र वणर
य नि वश्ेमनि काः॥11-16॥

11.16.sanskrit:
स्वयमिन्दिरैव पतिमानकरी गृहकर्म यत्र कुरुते सुतराम् ।
अपि किङ्करीदशशतीमहिता श्रियमत्र वर्णयति वेश्मनि कः॥ १६ ॥
11.16.english:
Who can depict in full, the majesty of this imposing mansion where
Goddess Mahalakshmi with thousands of servants waiting on her, attends
personally to her house-hold work and devotedly worships Lord Narayana?
(This brings out the supremacy of Sri Hari.)
कमला-पनिाः कमलराऽमलरा कमिीररा कमल-लोचिरा।
अमृिानह-राज-मृद-भोग-गिाः स्व-रिोऽनप ित्र रमिेपरमाः॥11-17॥

11.17.sanskrit:
कमलापतिः कमलयाऽमलया कमनीयया कमललोचनया ।
अमृताहिराजमृदुभोगगतः स्वरतोऽपि तत्र रमते परमः ॥ १७ ॥
11.17.english:
In this pleasure dome, Sri Hari the supreme, though pleased with his
own self, sports with his chaste and charming lotus-eyed consort
Mahalakshmi (for her delight), reclining on his cushion bed, the soft body of
the liberated serpent sovereign.
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 172

िरुण-प्रभा-कर-सहस्र-रुचंकिकाम्बरंमनण-मराभरणम।्
हनसिाििन्दे मरनवन्द-दृशंिमरीन्द्र-धानरणमि-ुस्मरि॥11-18॥

11.18.english:
(Oh Sanaka and other sages) remember ever, the lotus-eyed Lord
Narayana, holding the discus in his hand, commanding the lustre of a
thousand suns, robed in golden (yellow) garments, decked with ornaments
in-laid with variegated diamonds, with his moon-like radiant face charming
with its pleasant smile.
गणरदे ्रमाऽस्य ि गणुािनिलाि च् िरुाििश्चिरुवागनप िो।
ि वरंसहस्र-वदिा अनप िाि अ् परोनदिािनि-वदमे पिु
ाः॥11-19॥

11.19.sanskrit:
गणयेद्रमाऽस्य न गुणानखिलांश्चतुराननश्चतुरवागपि नो ।
न वयं सहस्रवदना अपि तानपरोदितानतिवदेम पुनः ॥ १९ ॥
11.19.english:
The serpent-Lord continues: ‘Even Goddess Mahalakshmi cannot
reckon the attributes of Lord Narayana in their entirety, neither
Chaturmukha-Brahma eloquent in speech, nor myself gifted with a thousand
mouths (hoods) but nevertheless can narrate more emphatically the same
qualities delineated by Sages (like Shuka and others).
नवधरो नवहङ्ग-पिरोऽगनणिा रमणीरिुाश्च फनणपन्द्रे -मिााः ु ।
पर-रोग-भनूर-सिु -भोग-नमिा अवलोक्य िंदधनि मोद-रसम॥्11-20॥

11.20.sanskrit:
विधयो विहङ्गपतयोऽगणिता रमणीयुताश्च फणिपेन्द्रमुखाः ।
परयोगभूरिसुखभोगमिता अवलोक्य तं दधति मोदरसम्॥ २० ॥
11.20.english:
Countless Brahmas, Garudas (Guardian of Birds), Sheshas
(Sovereigns of snakes), Indras (God of Gods) and others along with their
queens discern Lord Narayana (with deep devotion) and experience the joy
born of divine contemplation and taste the essence of bliss eternal.
स-चिभुज
य
ु ा विज-पत्र-दृशो वर-वनेषणोऽत्र स-नपशङ्ग-पटााः।
अरुण-प्रभाश्च िरुणाभ्र-रुचो नवचरनन्त िार्-रुचराः परुुषााः॥11-21॥

11.21.sanskrit:
सचतुर्भुजा वनजपत्रदृशो वरवेषिणोऽत्र सपिशङ्गपटाः ।
अरुणप्रभाश्च तरुणाभ्ररुचो विचरन्ति नाथरुचयः पुरुषाः॥ २१ ॥
11.21.english:
Such of the released souls who have secured salvation of Sarupya
type sport in the vast ground of in this celestial city (Vaikunta), endowed with
four arms, lotus (lovely) eyes, dark-blue hue like that of a new-born cloud,
decked with fascinating ornaments and donned in rich (yellow) raiments
glistening with the glory of the rising Sun. (They attain some similarity, albeit,
never identity with Lord Narayana.)
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 173

भगवि-्समीपमपु-रािविांप्रमदो ि केवलमलब्ध-िलु
ाः।
अनप िस्य लोकमनि-लोक-गणु ंभजिांस-ुदल
य
भमभनूर-शभु
ाः
ै॥11-22॥

11.22.sanskrit:
भगवत्समीपमुपयातवतां प्रमदो न केवलमलब्धतुलः ।
अपि तस्य लोकमतिलोकगुणं भजतां सुदुर्लभमभूरिशुभैः॥ २२ ॥
11.22.english:
The felicity of those enfranchised souls who attain the proximity of the
Lord (Samipya type of Mukti) alone, is not unique (unparalleled); the
beatitude in Vaikunta transcending the bliss attainable in Satya-Loka,
inaccessible to the less meritorious, is entire (full) but varies in extent with
the fitness of the liberated souls who attain Sarupya, Samipya, Salokya and
Sayujjya (types of liberation).
ि भवनन्त रत्र मृनि-जन्म-जरााः नत्रनवधंभरंनकमिु दाःि-गणाः।
अपरंच नकनञ्चदशभुंि भविे ि् गणुानद िि-्प्रभव-मलू मनप॥11-23॥

11.23.sanskrit:
न भवन्ति यत्र मृतिजन्मजरास्त्रिविधं भयं किमुत दुःखगणः ।
अपरं च किञ्चिदशुभं न भवेन्न गुणादि तत्प्रभवमूलमपि॥ २३ ॥
11.23.english:
When birth, death and old age or the triple terrors (Adhyatmika,
Adhibhoutika, Adhidhaivika) find no place in this world of Vishnu, then of
what avail are the chain of other sorrows? (They also matter not.) Neither
any other blemishes (inauspicious qualities) like jealously, nor the qualities
of Sattva, Rajas, Tamas - the root cause of remorse dare soil its sanctity. (It
is a supreme Loka (world) full of joy and shorn of sorrows.)
अि-ुभरूिेसिु मिन्त-सिाः
ै
सकलर
ै
लं-मनि-पदंसििम।्
अनप िारिम्य-सनहिैाः स्व-गरुु-प्रवणाः
ै
परस्परमरुु-प्रणरैाः॥11-24॥

11.24.sanskrit:
अनुभूयते सुखमनन्तसखैः सकलैरलम्मतिपदं सततम् ।
अपि तारतम्यसहितैः स्वगुरुप्रवणैः परस्परमुरुप्रणयैः ॥ २४ ॥
11.24.english:
All the released souls who regard Lord Narayana as their unrelated
relative, (who bestows favours) take gradation for granted (do not question)
and abide in the world of Vishnu in fraternal cordiality (not jealous of each
other), with full devotion and respect (bear no malice) for their superiors,
ever enjoying divine bliss to satiety.
अनि-सन्दु रााः सरुभराः परुुषााः हनर-चन्दििे िव-चन्द्र-रुचा।
चल-चारु-चामर-धराि-ुचरा नवहरनन्त नित्य-िरुणा इह ि॥े 11-25॥

11.25.sanskrit:
अतिसुन्दराः सुरभयः पुरुषा हरिचन्दनेन नवचन्द्ररुचा ।
चलचारुचामरधरानुचरा विचरन्ति नित्यतरुणा इह ते ॥ २५ ॥
11.25.english:
These freed souls ever young, handsome, smelling sweet being
smeared with red sandal paste, sport at will in Vaikunta fanned by
attendants with charming chouries.
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 174

स-नवलास-लास्य-पनरिष्टु-दृशो मृद-गीि-वाद्य-मनुदि-श्रवणााः।
अि-ुरञ्जरनन्त िव-कञ्ज-दृशो रमणा अमी स्व-रमणीस्तरुणीाः॥11-26॥

11.26.sanskrit:
सविलासलास्यपरितुष्टदृशो मृदुगीतवाद्यमुदितश्रवणाः ।
अनुरञ्जयन्ति नवकञ्जदृशो रमणा अमी स्वरमणीस्तरुणीः॥ २६ ॥
11.26.english:
(In Vaikunta), the ever-green (young) husbands with their eyes
feasting on the fascinating dances full of sportive (bodily) gestures and their
ears bewitched with the dulcet songs and magical harmony of the musical
instruments, sympathetically entertain their lotus-eyed consorts.
पनरणारकािपु-विानभ-मिु ाि अ् िुनिस्सरनन्त हनरणी-िरिााः।
नवमलाद ्नवमाि-वलराच्छिकैाः शनश-मडिलानदव रुचो रुनचरााः॥11-27॥

11.27.sanskrit:
परिणायकानुपवनाभिमुखाननु निःसरन्ति हरिणीनयनाः ।
विमलाद्विमानवलयाच्छनकैः शशिमण्डलादिव रुचो रुचिराः॥ २७ ॥
11.27.english:
The lovely ladies having charming deer-like eyes come out of their
clean cluster of celestial aeroplanes (vimanas) like the rays of the moon
moving out of its disc and slowly accompany their husbands on their way to
the supernal park.
अरुणाश्म-वण-य
ििवस्तिवाः शभु-हार-सनन्नभ-रुचोऽप्यपरााः।
हनरिील-िील-रुचरोऽत्र परााः प्रकटीभवनन्त नवनवधाभरणााः॥11-28॥

11.28.sanskrit:
अरुणाश्मवर्णतनवस्तनवः शुभहारसन्निभरुचोऽत्र पराः ।
हरिनीलनीलरुचयोऽप्यपराः प्रकटीभवन्ति विविधाभरणाः॥ २८ ॥
11.28.english:
These winning women of slender frames that appear there,
embellished with varied ornaments are of captivating complexions, some red
like rubies, others white like the diamond necklace, while still others bear the
semblance of the blue-sapphires.
सनुचर-द्यिुीदध
य नय
ि सक्ष्मू -िरंस्वरमम्बरंच िलु नबभ्रनि रााः।
शकु -निाःस्विा ध्रवुनममा अपरााः स-ुपरो-धरा जल-धरावलराः॥11-29॥

11.29.sanskrit:
सुचिरद्युतीर्दधति सूक्ष्मतरं स्वयमम्बरं च खलु बिभ्रति याः ।
शुकनिस्वना ध्रुवमिमा अपराः सुपयोधरा जलधरावलयः ॥ २९ ॥
11.29.english:
These lovely ladies of lasting lustre (aged sixteen) donned in fine,
dainty costumes, with beautiful breasts and sweet-throated like parrots,
verily seem like another row of rain-bearing clouds, (yet at variance with their
fleeting flare, dissonant din and strange apparel. (A young lady sixteen years
of age is named Jaldharavali – Bhavprakashika.)
समिीि-नवर्द्ु
म-भवुांस-ुदृशांपद-पल्लवाग्र्य-िि-रक्त-रुचीाः।
पर-भागिाः स्फनटक-कुनट्टम-भाः
ूप्रकटीकरोनि ि परुा प्रकटााः॥11-30॥
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 175

11.30.sanskrit:
समतीतविद्रुमभुवां सुदृशां पदपल्लवाग्रयनखरक्तरुचीः ।
परभागतः स्फटिककुट्टिमभूः प्रकटीकरोति न पुरा प्रकटाः॥ ३० ॥
11.30.english:
The red gleam of the glistening nails of the tender feet of the damsels
with bewitching glances, (that pass unnoticed) while tripping the ruby
pavement, is thrown in bold relief (become manifest) while crossing the
white-hued (variegated) crystal floor.
हनरणी-दृशांनि-वसिानि बहृ ि-्स-ुनििम्ब-नबम्ब-रुनचरोरु-रुचा।
शबळ-द्यिुीनि मदिस्य जग-ज्जर-वज
ै
रन्त्य इनि निनश्चिमु
ाः॥11-31॥

11.31.sanskrit:
हरिणीदृशां सुवसनानि बृहत्सुनितम्बबिम्बरुचिरोरुरुचा ।
शबलद्युतीनि मदनस्य जगज्जयवैजयन्त्य इति निश्चिनुमः ॥ ३१ ॥
11.31.english:
We take for granted that the pleasant glow of the lovely hip-region of
the deer-eyed bevy of beauties mingled with the multi-colours of their (rich)
clothings looked like unfurled victory-banners of the God of Love
vanquishing the world of lovers. (Disarming the admirers.)
कर-पल्लवस्त
ै
रनळिल
ै यनलिाः
ै
असकृि क्व् णि-्किक-कङ्कणैाः।
िव-रोम-रानज-वनल-वल्ग-ुमृद-प्र-ििदूरर
ैनि-मिाक्िरळ
ै
ाः॥11-32॥
(Verse 32-35 to be read together, as they form an antya-Kulaka)

11.32.english:
These women, that have soft, active (moving) charming (sprout-like)
hands adorned with golden bangles twinkling time and again and slender,
tender bellies looking pretty with their three fine folds and budding lines of
hair that moved lightly (with their pretty movements).
कुच-कुा-पणू -य
िर-कानन्त-सधुा-रस-शषे-नबन्द-नवसर-च्छनवनभाः।
वर-हार-रानजनभरुिाि-ुपदंपनर-लोनठिानभरशिैरुरनस॥11-33॥

11.33.english:
That possess precious pearl-necklaces freely undulating on their
bosoms at every step, that seemed like shining nectarine drops dripping
from their over-full lustrous protuberant breasts.
अनप लीलरा कुवलरोल्लनसिाः
ै
अपराः
ै
कराः
ै
सहचरी-निनहिैाः।
वदिाः
ै
शनुच-नस्मि-कटाक्ष-रसैाः चल-कु
डिलोल्लनसि-गडि-रगु
ाः
ै॥11-34॥

11.34.sanskrit:
अपि लीलया कुवलयोल्लसितैरपरैः करैः सहचरीनिहितैः ।
वदनैः शुचिस्मितकटाक्षरसैश्चलकुण्डलोल्लसितगण्डयुगैः॥ ३४ ॥
11.34.english:
With their right hands glistening lily-blue picked for jest and the left
hands stretched over the shoulders of their companions with their charming
cheeks decked with pendant ear-rings and their faces adorned with gentle
smiles and enchanting side-glances.
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 176

प्रगिासुिासुरनणि
ै
रशिामनण-िपूरुनै
रव परुा गनदिााः।
अि-ुवीनक्षिंुिि-ुनवलासनममंिरुणा भवन्त्यर् निवत्तृ -मिु ााः॥11-35॥

11.35.sanskrit:
प्रगतासु तासु रणितैरशनामणिनूपुरैरिव तदा गदिताः ।
अनुवीक्षितुं तनुविलासमिमं तरुणा भवन्त्यथ निवृत्तमुखाः॥ ३५ ॥
11.35.english:
Alighted from their celestial chariots and jaunted ahead while their
sprightly (young) consorts turn back their faces as if beckoned by the
sportive movements of the bodies of their wives that bear jingling girdles and
jewelled anklets.
अि-ुपानलिााः स्वदनरिद
ैनय
रिााः कर्मप्प्प्यपुत्ये कुच-भार-ििााः।
शिक
ै
ाः पिुस्तदवलम्ब-बलाि न्कल िा नवशनन्त परमोप-विम॥्11-36॥

11.36.sanskrit:
अनुपालिताः स्वदयितैर्दयिताः कथमप्युपेत्य कुचभारनताः ।
शनकैः पुनस्तदवलम्बबलात्किल ता विशन्ति परमोपवनम्॥ ३६ ॥
11.36.english:
These beloveds bent by their heavy breasts and (hence) moving with
a slow gait somehow approach their awaiting sweet-hearts who have moved
ahead and with their help (hand-in-hand) slowly enter the angelic bowers.
हनसि-प्रसिू-निकरोरु-भर-प्रणमि-्सवुण-यमनण-सवि
य
रि।्
उपगहूरन्नमिृ -नसध-ुसिाः पविो ि कस्य सिु मधे रनि॥11-37॥

11.37.english:
Who will not feel more happy with the wind, the ally of the two nectaroceans (Ara and Nya in Brahma Loka), embracing the golden and jewelled
trees bent with the burden of rows of fully-bloomed flowers?
उपचारिन्दिनवन्दंद्यवुिंनिरमिे िन्दरनि रन्न जिम।्
परमवे िन्दिमपुन्द्रे विंसकलंसदा रदनभिन्दरनि॥11-38॥

11.38.sanskrit:
उपचारनन्दनमिदं द्युवनं नियमेन नन्दयति यन्न जनम् ।
परमेव नन्दनमुपेन्द्रवनं सकलं सदा यदभिनन्दयति ॥ ३८ ॥
11.38.english:
The garden in Indra-Loka though reckoned as Nandana-Vana is not
so in its primary sense (really) as it does not delight one and all as a rule
while the grove of Upendra (Narayana) in Vaikunta which invariably
exhilarates all its inmates (visitors) at all times, is the veritable supernal park
(Nandana-Vana) and hence surpasses it in splendour and excellence.
वर-पानरभर्द्क-स-ुकल्पिरु-व्रज-पानरजाि-हनर-चन्दि-वि।्
सनहिंसमानद-पद-िाििगाः
ै
पनरभषूरनन्त िदहो ऋिवाः॥11-39॥

11.39.english:
It is the wonderful pleasure-haunt (strange-land) enjoying all the
seasons at all times, embellished with the excellent five kinds of heavenly
trees named Paribhadraka (Mandara), Kalpa-vriksha, Parijata,
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 177

Harichandana and Santana Vriksha adorned with seasonal flowers and
fruits.
नस्मि-माधवी-कुसमु-रम्य-िरौ िव-चम्पकानद-कुसमुोल्लनसिौ।
मध-ुमाधवौ मधपु-गीमध
य
रुौ मधनुजि-्नप्रराि प्र्मदमािरिाः॥11-40॥

11.40.english:
In this retreat the Vasanta season comprising of the months of
Chaitra and Vaisakha, with its great charm of the full-blown flowers of the
Madhavi trees, decked by the fresh champaka and other fragrant flowers
and buzzing with the sweet hum of the bees, gladdens the dear devotees of
Sri Hari.
िव-मनल्लका-स्रजमभीष्ट-िमाः शनुच-सावांप्रणनरिी-नशरनस।
कलरि स्व् रंि कुशलेनि नकल प्रनिघट्टरत्यरु-कुचावरुसा॥11-41॥

11.41.sanskrit:
नवमल्लिकास्रजमभीष्टतमः शुचिसम्भवां प्रणयिनीशिरसि ।
कलयन् स्वयं न कुशलेति किल प्रतिघट्टयत्युरुकुचावुरसा॥ ४१ ॥
11.41.english:
In this merry mood, the devoted husband jocosely jesting ‘you cannot
do justice to it’, decently decks the head (the plait of hair) himself with a
wreath of fresh Jasmine flowers available (born) in the month of Jyesta and
Ashada. (Under this pretext, he closely clasps to his chest the bulging
breasts of his dear darling.)
मधरु-स्वरास्ति-पित्र-िनि-प्रकटान्त-पनि-धिृ -ित्रे-रुचाः।
नशनििोऽि-ुरानन्त नह सहस्र-दृशंजलदि-य
ुलक्ष्म-निज-ित्तृ महे॥11-42॥
11.42. The peacocks, gifted with a sonorous voice in the flush of their
dancing festivity, heralding the advent of Shravana and Bhadrapada with the
lines of circular dots seen in the tips of the cluster of the spread-out feathers,
resemble the thousand eyed Indra.
शरदोल्लसि-्कुवलरक्षे णरा नवकचारनवन्द-रुनचराििरा।
नप्रररा च शोभि-शकु -स्विरा रमिेजिो मिु र-हंसकरा॥11-43॥

11.43.sanskrit:
शरदोल्लसत्कुवलयेक्षणया विकचारविन्दरुचिराननया ।
प्रियया च शोभनशुकस्वनया रमते जनो मुखरहंसकया ॥ ४३ ॥
11.43.english:
The released souls elated with the luxury of autumn, revel with their
beloveds that have enchanting (blue water-lily like) eyes, fascinating (fullblown lotus-like) faces, parrot-like melodious throats and (decorated with)
jingling anklets.
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 178

अनभिन्दरि न्हमऋिंुस-ुसिा स्फनटकाचल-च्छल-नहमानधकृिम।्
फनलिी-कुलंकुसमुरि न्शनशरो हसिीव पल्लु -िव-कुन्द-रुचा॥11-44॥

11.44.sanskrit:
अभिनन्दयन् हिमॠतुं सुसखा स्फटिकाचलच्छलहिमाधिकृतम् ।
फलिनीकुलं कुसुमयञ्छिशिरो हसतीव फुल्लनवकुन्दरुचा ॥ ४४ ॥
11.44.english:
The coming of the cold (Shishir) season; that pleases its ally the
winter-season with the snow spread-out on all sides appearing like a hill clad
in white-crystal, blossoms the flowers in the thickets and looks as if laughing
with the brightness of the fresh-bloomed Kunda flowers.
नहम-शीि-वष-यपनरिाप-परुस्सर-दोष-मलू नमह ििग
य
ुणाः।
नप्ररमवे साधरनि नित्यमहो स नवमनुक्तमानिनि नवमनुक्तमिाम॥्11-45॥

11.45.sanskrit:
हिमशीतवर्षपरितापपुरस्सरदोषमूलमिह नर्तुगणः ।
प्रियमेव साधयति नित्यमहो स विमुक्तिमानिति विमुक्तिमताम् ॥ ४५ ॥
11.45.english:
It is verily surprising that all the (six) seasons, here (in Vishnu-loka)
inclusive of winter, cold, rainy season and summer that have attained
salvation being freed from their respective faults like snow, rain and heat,
render satisfaction to the emancipated souls.
अमृि-स्रगत्तुम-फल-प्रसवाि अ् मिृ ार ित्र जििाऽपर
य नि।
उप-भोक्तुमप्यनवनध-बध-वशा प्रकृनिंप्र-रानि सकलोऽनप सदा॥11-46॥

11.46.sanskrit:
अमृतस्रगुत्तमफलप्रसवानमृताय तत्र जनताऽर्पयति ।
उपभोक्तुमप्यविधिबन्धवशा प्रकृतिं प्रयाति सकलोऽपि सदा ॥ ४६ ॥
11.46.english:
The enfranchised souls in Vaikunta, though not liable to any
commandments (laid down in Shrutis and Smritis), voluntarily offer with
intense devotion nectar, garlands, delicious fruits and flowers to the eternal
and independent Lord Narayana and then enjoy them (as they used to do in
mundane living); Oh, How hard it is to hide one’s nature. (People ever follow
their own nature.)
प्रमदानि-रके मपु-रािविा प्रमदा-गणिे चरिोपवि।े
उप-गीरिेस्म मधरुंमध-ुनजच्छनरिंस-कान्त-िनििा सििम॥्11-47॥

11.47.sanskrit:
प्रमदातिरेकमुपयातवता प्रमदागणेन चरतोपवने ।
उपगीयते स्म मधुरं मधुजिच्चरितं सकान्तततिना सततम् ॥ ४७ ॥
11.47.english:
In this Vaikunta, the bevy of beauties sport with their beloveds in this
(luxuriant) garden in great delight and in an exultant mood (out of excessive
joy) sing in sonorous tunes the great glories of Lord Sri-Hari.
ललिा ललन्त्यनभवरंप्रवरा लनळिा लुिनन्त कुसमुानि परााः।
इिरााः स्रजो नव-रचरनन्त पिीि अ् ि-ुरञ्जरनन्त ि नह काश्चि िो॥11-48॥
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 179

(In Vishnu-loka) some lovely ladies dance before their consorts, while a few
other tender-bodied pick the flowers while still other (sweet-hearts) weave
garlands, thus all of them invariably entertain their lords.
वि-लीलरा नवनवधरा मनुदिााः िरुणा घिस्ति-ििास्तरुणीाः।
स-ुलिालरषे ुपनररभ् रनिंवदिारनवन्द-मधपुा दधनि॥11-49॥

11.49.sanskrit:
वनलीलया विविधया मुदितास्तरुणा घनस्तननतास्तरुणीः ।
सुलतालयेषु परिरभ्य रतिं वदनारविन्दमधुपा दधति ॥ ४९ ॥
11.49.english:
The Adams (young men) delighted with diverse diversions, engage in
amorous amusements in the beautiful bowers embracing their (wives) bend
with their protuberant breasts, sucking the sweetness (honey) from their
lotus-like lips.
रनसकााः सधुािुसरसीष ुििो रुि-िपूरुािकुृि-सारनसकााः।
नवहरनन्त साकमवगाह्य धवैाः जल-मड्डकु ोत्सव-हसद-्वदिााः॥11-50॥

11.51.sanskrit:
विविधा विधाय विहृतीः सलिले विकटेषु विद्रुमतटेषु गताः ।
सुरभिं सुवर्णमथ वर्णकमाददते सखीकरगतं महिलाः ॥ ५१ ॥
11.51.english:
Satiated with their varied water-gambols, the lovely ladies stand on
the broad coral shores (full of trees bearing reddish precious gems) and take
the fragrant unguents from the palms of their friends (to paste on their
bodies).
जघिाशं कु
ै
रवरवाभरणाः
ै
कुच-कुङ्कुमश्च
ै
कच-माल्य-वरैाः।
िरिाञ्जिैरनप नमर्ोऽब्ज-दृशांघनटिर
ै
लङ्कृनिरलं नक्ररि॥े 11-52॥

11.52.sanskrit:
जघनांशुकैरवयवाभरणैः कुचकुंकुमैश्च कचमाल्यवरैः ।
नयनाञ्जनैरपि मिथोऽब्जदृशां घटितैरलंकृतिरलं क्रियते ॥ ५२ ॥
11.52.english:
The mutual co-ordination, between the loins of the beautiful lotuseyed women and their costumes, their various limbs and their varied
embellishments, their breasts and their saffron, their plaits and their wreath,
their eyes and the collyrium, is enticing enough to enhance their excellence
(charm).
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 180

ििमुध्यमा निज-गहृ ानण गिााः सममात्मिामस-ुसमैरसमैाः।
अमिृ ंनपबन्त्यसलुभंभनवनभाः चषक
ै
मह
य
ा-रजि-रत्-मरैाः॥11-53॥

11.53.sanskrit:
तनुमध्यमा निजगृहाणि गताः सममात्मनामसुसमैरसमैः ।
अमृतं पिबन्त्यसुलभं भविभिश्चषकैर्महारजतरत्नमयैः ॥ ५३ ॥
11.53.english:
Returning to their residences, the darlings with slender waists quaff
with their dear-ones, precious as their very life-breath yet superior to them,
ambrosia from golden goblets studded with gems, not available to the
mortals (Samsarins).
मृद-कम्र-शय्य-मिरुागविांमनण-मञ्चमनञ्चि-दृशोऽवसर।े
अनप चोनदिा अनध-रुहनन्त शिाः
ैनविरो नह भषूरनि सव-य
रसम॥्11-54॥

11.54.sanskrit:
मृदुकम्रशय्यमनुरागवतां मणिमञ्चमञ्चितदृशोऽवसरे ।
पतिचोदिता अधिरुहन्ति शनैः विनयो हि भूषयति सर्वरसम् ॥ ५४ ॥
11.54.english:
The damsels with decorated eyes leisurely mount the cushioned and
charming beds spread on the diamond cots of their loving lords at the proper
moment, prompted by their partners. Verily, modesty is an embellishment to
all sentiments (like Srinagara-love).
अनभकान्तमन्नुनमि-कान्त-कुचा नकल बर्ध्िी स-कुसमुांकबरीम।्
समुिु ी स-ुलक्ष्य-भजु -मलू -वनलाः पनर-रभ्िेप्रणनरिाऽनि-रसाि॥्11-55॥

11.55.sanskrit:
अभिकान्तमुन्नमितकान्तकुचा किल बध्नती सकुसुमां कबरीम् ।
सुमुखी सुलक्ष्यभुजमूलवलिः परिरभ्यते प्रणयिनाऽतिरसात् ॥ ५५ ॥
11.55.english:
A lovely lady facing her dear devoted is embraced by him in earnest
endearment lured by her elevated, enticing breasts while tying her braid
decked with flowers with the folds in her armpit distinctly discerned.
पर्ृ -ुवृत्त-िङ्गु-कनठिोरनसज-िर-वानरिारि-निजान्तररोाः।
भजुरोरग
य
ुिे रमणो रमणीं पनररभ् कोऽत्र रमरन्ने सिु ी॥11-56॥

11.56.sanskrit:
पृथुवृत्ततुङ्गकठिनोरसिजद्वयवारितायतनिजान्तरयोः ।
भुजयोर्युगेन रमणो रमणीं परिरभ्य कोऽत्र रमयेन्न सुखी॥ ५६ ॥
11.56.english:
Which gay husband in Vaikunta will not give happiness to his beloved
by hugging her closely with his two arms and broad chest impeded by her
pair of broad, round, solid and elevated breasts?
नस्मि-पल्लु मल्लु नसि-निनल्ल-लसि-्मनण-कु
डिलंमदृ-कटाक्ष-पट
ु
।
निभिृ ो निरीक्ष्य स-ुनचरंस-ुििोाः प्रनि-चम्बु नि नप्रर-िमो वदिम॥्11-57॥

11.57.sanskrit:
स्मितफुल्लमुल्लसितचिल्लिलसन्मणिकुण्डलं मृदुकटाक्षपटु ।
निभृतो निरीक्ष्य सुचिरं सुतनोः प्रतिचुम्बति प्रियतमो वदनम्॥ ५७ ॥
11.57.english:
The devoted lover discerning with a steady gaze, for a pretty long
time his darling’s delightful face blooming with a pleasant smile, her beaming
cheeks glistening with the diamond ear-rings, and her enchanting eyes with
their tender and smart side-glances, closely kisser her.
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 181

ििमन्नुमय्य मिु -चन्द्रमसंपनररा-साृि-मदुा स-ुदृशाः।
उप-रम्यिेि िरुणिे नचरंमधरुाधरामिृ -रसंनपबिा॥11-58॥

11.58.sanskrit:
नतमुन्नमय्य मुखचन्द्रमसं परिरम्भसम्भृतमुदा सुदृशः ।
उपरम्यते न तरुणेन चिरं मधुराधरामृतरसं पिबता ॥ ५८ ॥
11.58.english:
The young lover, greatly gratified (full of joy) with un-interrupted
embrace of his beloved with bewitching eyes, lifting her face lowered down
with shame, drinks the nectarine essence of her sweet lower-lip (kisses her
again and again).
सरुिोत्सवोत्सकु िरा त्वररि करपल्लवं निवसिग्रहणे ् ।
कररुद्धमारिदृशाः सहसा त्वररत्यर् निगणु नमष्टिमाः॥11-59॥

11.59.sanskrit:
सुरतोत्सवोत्सुकतया त्वरयन् करपल्लवं निवसनग्रहणे ।
कररुद्दमायतदृशः सहसा त्वरयत्यथ द्विगुणमिष्टतमः ॥ ५९ ॥
11.59.english:
Thus, the husband enamoured with intense pious passion, hastening
to disrobe his broad-eyed beloved with his winning (sprout-like) hands when
hampered by her, doubles his zeal. (Force)
रभसावपानिि-पर्ृ रूनसजाः पनर-रभ् िि-्क्षण-नवराि-िराः।
मनणिानद-सौरि-नविाि-पट
ू
रमरत्यमिू र् वुनि-सङ् उि॥11-60॥

11.60.sanskrit:
रभसावपातितपृथूरसिजः परिरभ्य तत्क्षणवियाततरः ।
मणितादिसौरतवितानपटू रमयत्यमून् युवतिसङ्घ उत॥ ६० ॥
11.60.english:
He further alerts the disputant not to avow that Ishwara though
endued with the knowledge of Form and the like, is not endowed with
happiness because he will have to taste unhappiness also, since the
pervasion of pleasure and pain has already been denied.
दृष्टान्तो रस्य िष्टेोऽरंिस्य स्याद ्व्यनिरके वाि।्
नशलावनदत्यिाः शद्धु-नचद-्दहेेनन्द्रर-भोग्रसौ॥15-61॥

11.61.sanskrit:
अतिलौकिकान् रमयतो रमणान् रमयन्त्यलौकिकगुणा ललनाः ।
अनुरूपरूपमतिलोकसुखं तदलौकिकं निधुवनं लसति ॥ ६१ ॥
11.61.english:
The ladies in Vaikunta, dowered with exceptional qualities not to be
evinced in other worlds, gladden the equally strange lovers who greatly
gratify them. Their sexual enjoyment born of mutual acquiescence,
surpasses the merriments elsewhere (in other worlds) being exquisite (an
embellishment) in its excellence. (Bereft of blemishes)
ि रनिाः श्रमानद-िनचिा निरमाद ्नवरह-व्यर्ाऽनप ि वध-ूवररोाः।
प्रनिकानमिी-प्रणरिाः कलहो ि समनस्त दोष-रनहिेनह पद॥े 11-62॥

11.62.sanskrit:
न रतिः श्रमादिखचिता नियमाद्विरहव्यथाऽपि न वधूवरयोः ।
प्रतिकामिनीप्रणयतः कलहो न समस्तदोषरहिते हि पदे ॥ ६२ ॥
11.62.english:
In this divine world (Vaikunta unconcerned with Prakrati, hence
faultless) divorced from mundane defects, sexual sports naturally enough
are disassociated with sweat and free from fatigue; neither do the pair suffer
TOC http://srimadhvyasa.wordpress.com/ https://sites.google.com/site/srimadhvyasa/
आचारााःय श्रीमदाचारााःय सन्तुमेजन्म जन्मनि॥गरुुभावंव्यञ्जरनन्त भानि श्री जरिीर्वय ाक्॥कृष्णंवन्देजगद्गरुुम ॥् Page 182

the pangs of separation nor are they contentious (pick quarrels with their
lords) or jealous of their co-wives.
सिु -सनंवदात्मक-िरा वपषुांसरुनभत्वमवे बनहरन्तरनप।
हनर-धानम्न दम्पनि-गणस्य ििो ििुमान्यिऽेनप मनुिनभाः सरुिम॥्11-63॥
